characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: A brilliant attorney who wins her high-stakes lawsuits byany meansnecessary. Hewes is a tempered-steelIron Lady, but still manages to frequently surprise with the depths she'll sink to toPay Evil unto Evil. Her backstory and true motivations are slowly revealed as the series progresses. Could easily be the poster child forAmoral Attorney, and in any other show, would be the villain. Affably Evil: Amoral and manipulative as she may be, Patty is charming as hell. She can also be genuinely good to her people for al her verbal abuse, and tries to compensate for the worst of her actions. Amoral Attorney: She will cross ANY line to win a case. Ambiguously Evil: A running question throughout the series. She's clearly not a good person, but how much herPet the Dogmoments are genuine or fake is deliberately unclear. As well, is she aWell-Intentioned Extremisttrying to take down corrupt captains of industry or just as bad as her enemies? Anti-Hero: Patty is often up against very corrupt people, but goes to horrifying lengths to win.Arguably aVillain Protagonistfrom the start she certainly becomes this as the series progresses

characters.name: Patty Hewes
characters.description: A brilliant attorney who wins her high-stakes lawsuits byany meansnecessary. Hewes is a tempered-steelIron Lady, but still manages to frequently surprise with the depths she'll sink to toPay Evil unto Evil. Her backstory and true motivations are slowly revealed as the series progresses. Could easily be the poster child forAmoral Attorney, and in any other show, would be the villain. Affably Evil: Amoral and manipulative as she may be, Patty is charming as hell. She can also be genuinely good to her people for al her verbal abuse, and tries to compensate for the worst of her actions. Amoral Attorney: She will cross ANY line to win a case. Ambiguously Evil: A running question throughout the series. She's clearly not a good person, but how much herPet the Dogmoments are genuine or fake is deliberately unclear. As well, is she aWell-Intentioned Extremisttrying to take down corrupt captains of industry or just as bad as her enemies? Anti-Hero: Patty is often up against very corrupt people, but goes to horrifying lengths to win.Arguably aVillain Protagonistfrom the start she certainly becomes this as the series progresses

characters.name: Ellen Parsons
characters.description: Begins the series as a fresh out of law school intern to Hewes and Associates, personally hired by Patty, who dreams of becoming a corporate lawer. Initially seeing Patty as a role model, she learns that there is a dark side to corporate litigation and that her new boss has no scruples when it comes to winning. Arguably the true protagonist of the series,the entirety of season 1shows her gradually learning the stakes and becoming achess masterin her own right. Ambition Is Evil: Ellen's entire problem in a nutshell; even though a sane person would have walked away from Patty after season one, Ellen's driving ambition to be the best sadly keeps her in Patty's orbit in order to be used by her. While Patty spends most of seasons two and three trying to get Ellen to embrace her destiny and let herself be groomed to run the law firm when Patty retires, by season four Patty realizes that Ellen will never bow down to her in order to "get her reward" and wants to outdo Patty (hence her taking on not-Blackwater). Brainy Brunette: A newly qualified but respectable lawyer. Break the Cutie: The entire first season is this for Ellen. Broken Bird: After David's death for sure.

characters.name: Tom Shayes
characters.description: Patty's partner in her firm and one of the most moral members of the main cast. Unlike Patty he doesn't get many of the high-profile cases because of his stern moral code. Butt-Monkey: Nothing goes right for Tom. Can't Get Away with Nuthin'For most seasons Tom is the seriesButt-Monkey. The one time he tries to playchess masterwith the Tobins (whom he despises for suckering him and countless individuals into losing their life savings in a ponzi scheme) ends with his murder. Cold-Blooded Torture:His death. Foil: He's one to Patty, being a basically decent person working for far less moral employers.

characters.name: Uncle Pete
characters.description: Patty's right hand man for several years. According to Patty he is her maternal uncle. Beware the Silly Ones: Uncle Pete initially seems to be just a friendly, goofy old man. Then he slits Katie's dog's throat on Patty's orders. Bungled Suicide: After he knows the FBI is on to him. Chekhov's Gunman: He's shown in the background for years. Clean Up Crew: His stated job for Patty.

characters.name: Felicia Marquand
characters.description: A mid-ranking employee. The Generic Guy: She's mostly characterized as being a stern professional, and lacks a lot of the quirks of the other members of the firm.

characters.name: Andrew Vida
characters.description: A mid-ranking employee Can't Take Criticism: His reaction to Ellen offering contradictory advice to what he just told Patty is to threaten her if she ever shows him up again. Mistaken for Gay: Patty teases him by claiming she thought he was this, after Andrew offhandedly mentions having a girlfriend.

characters.name: David Connor
characters.description: Ellen's fiance. He is a doctor and initially disapproves of Ellen joining Patty Hewes' law firm. Foregone Conclusion:His demise, also counts as aFirst-Episode Twist. Friendly Target: His torment and death triggers his fiancee's plot. Nice Guy: Is loyal to Ellen and good to his patients, even when he is the target of a stalker. Sacrificial Lion: His death in the first episode clearly kicks off the plot.

characters.name: Katie Connor
characters.description: David's sister and Ellen's future sister-in-law. Advertised Extra: After becoming a main cast member in season 2 she contributes a whole lot less to the plot than she did in season 1. ChekhovsGunwoman:Had a one night stand with a witness Patty wanted to testify against Frobisher. To get closer to the witness Patty hired Ellen, knowing that she was sister-in-law to be to Katie, and tried to get to the witness through her. Dumb Blonde: Subverted. Fake American: Anastasia Griffith is a British actress playing an American.

characters.name: Wes Krulick
characters.description: Aborted Arc: In the second season finale when hesaves Patty's lifeit seems to hint at the beginning of some sort of storyline more directly involving the two of them. Unfortunately, the potential arc got shelved thanks to Olyphant becoming unavailable for the third seasonother than a couple brief scenes in the finale.Similarly, his backstory: implied to have a personal reason to hate Frobrisher and his backstory (which falls underMultiple-Choice Past) has him either as a disgraced dirty ex-cop or a crook being blackmailed. Similarly, his backstory: implied to have a personal reason to hate Frobrisher and his backstory (which falls underMultiple-Choice Past) has him either as a disgraced dirty ex-cop or a crook being blackmailed. Anti-Villain: Starts out like thisuntil hisHeel–Face Turn. The Mole:For Rick Messer. Multiple-Choice Past: One episode implies that he's a dirty ex-cop who owes his freedom to Messer and another implies that he's a criminal that flipped into being his personal slave in exchange for him not arresting him for an unstated crime.

characters.name: Michael Hewes
characters.description: Patty's teenage son withDaniel Purcell. He is the closest thing to aMorality Petthat Patty has, although she is not above manipulating him for her own ends. Blackmail Backfire:What ultimately gets him killed. Despair Event Horizon:Tries to kill his mother after she has his girlfriend arrested for statutory rape Morality Pet: He's Patty's only child but her soft spot for him puts Michael firmly in this category. Yank the Dog's Chain: When Patty sends him off to a teen boot camp (via abduction) to "scare him straight", Michael actually comes to enjoy it. Then Patty threatens to cut off all financial support if he doesn't come back to New York City with her, being too much of aControl Freakto leave him there.

characters.name: Phil Grey
characters.description: Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: Phil is an avuncular man who comes off as much nicer than his wife and son, but he's eventually revealed to be aSerial Adulterer. Only Sane Man: Despite his adultery, Phil still comes off as more well-adjusted than Patty's criminality and Michael's teenage angst and clear struggles with mental illness. Serial Adulterer: He's been had many affairs over the years. Patty secretly knew about it and didn't particularly care, but divorces him only after he gets caught.

characters.name: Larry Popler
characters.description: The main representative of the workers suing Arthur Frobisher. Mistreatment-Induced Betrayal: He sides with Frobisher after Patty unilaterally turns down a $100 million settlement the man offered. Only in It for the Money: He makes it clear he just wants a payout from Frobisher to reimburse him and the other workers, and unlike Hewes & Associates, is not trying to pursue a vendetta against him.

characters.name: Daniel Purcell
characters.description: A respected scientist and former friend of Patty in the past. He becomes involved in Walter Kendrick's energy profiteering scam. He has his own secrets that he is trying to hide... Corrupt the Cutie: In his backstory with Patty. Happily Married:Subverted. He tried to kill his wife when she found out about his involvement with Kendrick. Unbeknownst to him, she was still alive when he left, only for a hitman hired by the Kendricks to come in and finish the job.) Luke, I Am Your Father:He's the biological father of Patty's son Michael. Real Life Writes the Plot: William Hurt didn't enjoy the fact that he frequently received only fractions of a script at a time which made it difficult for him to navigate his character's arc as he had never worked on a TV series before. This led to him being difficult to work with and caused his arc to be less prominent, with Marcia Gay Harden's character expanded to fill in his role in the plotline and the creation of a new villain in the form of Walter Kendrick.

characters.name: Arthur Frobisher
characters.description: A corrupt executive of a major company. Patty represents his former clients who were defrauded out of millions by his schemes. Affably Evil: Comes off as more of a thickheaded teenager who simply never grew up than really evil, and he's genuinely friendly and appreciative towards Ray. Although he transfers intoFaux Affably Evilby the end of Season 1. Big Bad: For season 1. Patty's lawsuit against him is what drives the plot, and Frobisher is the main obstacle to the protagonists. The Bus Came Back/Commuting on a Bus: The latter in season 2, the former in season 3. Clear My Name: His goal throughout the series is to clear his name from the accusations he committed fraud that left thousands jobless and broke. Of course, he is guilty, but is convinced otherwise.

characters.name: Ray Fiske
characters.description: Frobisher's lawyer and insider trader. Amoral Attorney: One of the milder ones in the series. He skews some ethical lines, but unlike Patty, Ray doesn't do anythingillegal. He's willing to defame Kathy, but never commits or is involved in any criminal activity. Anti-Villain: Throughout the season, he is haunted by dreams indicated his turmoil over this case. Armored Closet Gay: Eventually revealed to be this, though it does seem to be known to some people, specifically to Frobisher. Ate His Gun:How he commits suicide.

characters.name: Rick Messer
characters.description: A shady enforcer working for Frobisher. Beard of Evil: A ruthless hitman who sports a large beard. Dragon Their Feet: After the case with Frobisher is more or less resolved he spends the bulk of season 2 tying up his own loose ends related to the case. Internal Affairs:Messer spent several years working in the NYPD Internal Affairs division, where he used his position to protect dirty cops and create a network of friends who all owed him huge favors which he routinely cashed in for evil purposes. Names to Run Away from Really Fast: Messer, which is the German word for "knife".

characters.name: George Moore
characters.description: A shadowy associate of Frobisher. Bad Boss: He's very happy to use threats to keep his underlings in line, as his treatment of Gregory shows. He even has him beaten on several occasions and eventually puts a hit out on him. Corrupt Corporate Executive: A wealthy businessman involved in shady dealings and attempted murder. Deadpan Snarker: He has a bitterly caustic sense of humor. Faux Affably Evil: He acts charming and polite, but he'll very quickly get downright nasty if pressed even barely, while keeping the same soft-spoken tone.

characters.name: Gregory Malina
characters.description: Katie's lover, who is associated with Frobisher. Anti-Villain: He's a slimy coward, but he's also clearly in over his head and just trying to survive. Butt-Monkey: He's a weaselly coward, but Malina remains somewhat sympathetic because of how much abuse he takes. Being threatened and beaten by enemies and allies alike is routine for him. The Charmer: In a moment of vulnerable honestly, he admits to Ellen his only real talent is "getting people to like me". Dirty Coward: He's entirely concerned with saving his own hide and nothing else. He throws Katie to the wolves to protect himself, and later turns on Frobisher and Moore for the same reasons.

characters.name: George Berber
characters.description: An author hired to ghostwrite Frobisher's biography. Butt-Monkey: He's talked of dismissively by everyone behind his back, and winds up being verbally steamrolled by Frobisher in all of their conversations. It culminates in a drunken Frobisher breaking his nose in a fit of pique. The Dog Bites Back: Frobisher breaks his nose and fires him in a fit of spite. George promptly presses assault charges on him. Hidden Depths: When Frobisher arrives at his house impromptu in a drunken fit, George is revealed to have an immigrant girlfriend who travels the world, and who he seems genuinely devoted to. Nice Guy: He's a mild-mannered, polite man. He's diplomatic with Frobisher, and when the man shows up at his home drunk in the middle of the night, George tries to calmly talk him into leaving. He only gets angry when Frobisher makes racist comments about his girlfriend.

characters.name: Claire Maddox
characters.description: Ascended Extra: Originally supposed to be a minor character, her role was beefed up when Willaim Hurt's role was scaled back due to behind the scenes issues. The Rival: For Patty.

characters.name: Walter Kendrick
characters.description: Eviler than Thou: He's this to Arthur Frobisher. Put on a Bus:To prison. Real Life Writes the Plot: See this trope's entry under Daniel Purcell.

characters.name: Finn Garrity
characters.description: A whiz-kid mathematician. Chekhov's Gunman:He stabs Patti. Hookers and Blow: The two main components of hisSanity Slippage. Nerd in Evil's Helmet: Technically theBig Bad...but actually a nerdy child-genius mathematician. Psychopathic Man Child: As shown by his breakdowns throughout Season 2.

characters.name: Joe Tobin
characters.description: The Alcoholic: On the wagon at the start of season 3.FallsOff the Wagonspectacularly later on which leads to his downfall. Anti-Villain: Is a genuinely nice guy who is shocked at his father's misdeeds and just wants to his family outat first. Big Bad: Eventually. It's even him whokills Tom. Face–Heel Turn: Over the course of the season. Love Makes You Evil: His constant attempts to protect his family result in this.

characters.name: Lou Tobin
characters.description: Anti-Villain: A lot of what he did, includingsecretly keeping Danielle and Tessa Marchetti in his lifeand eventhe Ponzi schemebegan as attempts toshield his son from the consequences of potentially life-destroying mistakes. Knight Templar Parent: Eventually revealed to be one, as he attempted to protected Joe foryears. Parental Neglect: Zigzagged. Accused by his wife of making his son feel like "a ghost," but also went to great lengths to protect Joe and clean up his messes. Small Role, Big Impact: Only appears in a handful of scenes outside of the episode in which hekills himself, but his fraud and his neglect of his son are the causes of most of the bad stuff that goes down during the third season.

characters.name: Marilyn Tobin
characters.description: Driven to Suicide: She jumps off a bridge in the final episode of Season 3. Evil Matriarch: Ultimately, although it may be more of a case ofLove Makes You Evil, butgetting Joe to kill Tessa, his daughter and hergranddaughter, a fact she knewis thepoint of no return. I Was Quite a Looker: Mumbles something to this effect during the season finalejust before she commits suicide. My Beloved Smother: Towards Joe, who she loves more than anyone, but she's also extremely manipulative of him and ends up pushing him even further down the road ofSanity Slippageeven as she apparently tries to help him. Parental Favouritism: Joe. She goes to great lengths to reintegrate him into the family despite his alcoholism.

characters.name: Leonard Winstone
characters.description: Affably Evil: AnAmoral Attorney Amoral Attorney: He's worked for the Tobins. Dead Person Impersonation: Leonard Winstone isn't his real name. The Dog Bites Back: Despite lying about his identity, Lenny was loyal to the Tobins. Until Joe turned on him.Then he brought them all down and made off with a big chunk of their hidden fortune. Karma Houdini: He gets away with being a huge part in the Tobins' fraud, but he also pays deeply for it.

characters.name: Howard Erikson
characters.description: A private military contractor with lucrative US government contracts. Corrupt Corporate Executive: Though he's frustrated by the "corporate" part of his job. Howard views himself as a military leader that men are serving due to his own leadership and shared patriotism. In reality, his soldiers are employees, working for Howard purely because he pays well. Howard at one point snaps with rage fuelled physical violence when Chris mockingly points this out to him. Family-Values Villain: Gets some genuinePet the Dogmoments with his sons, whom he tries to raise properly. My Country, Right or Wrong: Almost outright stated. No one denied his patriotism, only his execution of that patriotism. Well, except for Boorman, but he's ajerk. Private Military Contractor: CEO of Blackwater Expy Highstar Security.

characters.name: Jerry Boorman
characters.description: A CIA agent who has collaborated with Howard Erikson on secret operations in Afghanistan. Big Bad:Responsible for much, if not all, of the mayhem in season 4. Evil Cannot Comprehend Good: Much of Season 4's plot happens because Jerry doesn't grasp the concept of loyalty.If he'd just left Chris alone, Chris would have probably kept his mouth shut out of respect for Erikson, and Ellen would have had no case. I Regret Nothing:His last words are "I'd do it again!" Morality Pet:Despite keeping him captive, Boorman is compassionate to the young Afghan boy. The child in question is the son of an Afghanistan female contact Boorman loved, With the season instigated by Boorman pulling one final mission to extract her, but instead finding the boy. It's likely the boy is Boorman's own son, and he's notably the one character that Boorman seems to still care for.

characters.name: Christopher Sanchez
characters.description: A high school classmate of Ellen's who works for Highstar Security.

characters.name: ChanningMcClaren
characters.description: AnExpyof Julian Assange and Edward Snowden who runs a Wikileaks-like website. Becomes Ellen's client in Season 5 Be as Unhelpful as Possible: Even though Ellen is his lawyer and required to keep all of his secrets, he can't stop deceiving her to save face, even when it hurts his case. Chick Magnet: Turns out a lot of ladies are drawn to a brooding rebel and global celebrity who looks likeRyan Phillippe. Mr. Fanservice: His bare chest and/or his butt makes it into frame almost once an episode. No Social Skills: Is bad with social cues and often offends people by accident.

characters.name: Rutger Simon
characters.description: A one-time old-school print journalist who now works as Channing'sNumber Two Cloudcuckoolander's Minder: His main role is to make sure the website runs smoothly despite Channing's undiagnosed mental disorder. Never Found the Body:The viewer watches him die, but the main characters never find out. Older Sidekick: Rutger sees himself as more of a partner than a sidekick, but this is how most people see him. The Resenter:His motive for betraying Channing and getting Naomi Walling killed.

characters.name: Kate Franklin
characters.description: A mysterious lawyer who joins Ellen's firm after years out of practice. Has a history with Patty. The Dutiful Daughter: Zigzagged. Kate and her sister both see themselves this way, but each was dutiful toward a different parent. Foil: To Patty.Appropriately enough, as they're sisters. The Maiden Name Debate: The fact that she took her husband's name is significant,because it hides the fact that her maiden name is 'Hewes,' and it helps underscore the differences between her and Patty.

characters.name: Hollis Nye
characters.description: An elderly lawyer and a friend of Ellen. Ignored Expert: He warns Ellen not to accept Patty's job offer because of her ruthlessness, and because her ego means she tends to hog any meaningful opportunities. Since there would be no show if she didn't accept, she doesn't follow his advice. The Mentor: Subverted. He's the typical mentor archetype, a wizened old man who gives advice to the rookie Ellen. However, throughout the first season she becomes increasingly under Patty's wing and loses contact with Hollis. Nice Guy: He's a kindly, grandfatherly sort.

characters.name: Martin Cutler
characters.description: Amoral Attorney: He's pretty blasé about defending a company that was responsible for the deaths of several people. Chekovs Gunman: Cutler is a minor character in the pilot, but he plays a major role in Tom's character arc in the first season when he tries to poach him from Hewes and Associates. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Disappears after Season 1. Smug Snake: He's a cocky, high-priced lawyer who finds himself easily manipulated and out-played by Patty. The Worf Effect: He serves this purpose in the pilot. He's a slick, high-priced lawyer who Patty effortlessly plays for a fool.

characters.name: Judge Toomey
characters.description: The judge supervising the Frobisher trial. Bald of Authority: A bald judge who is one of the few people who can make Patty listen to and follow his orders. Deadpan Snarker: He makes many jibes at Patty and Ray'sCourtroom Anticsand feuding. Reasonable Authority Figure: He's a fair and diplomatic man. Despite his sarcasm, he stops Patty and Fiske from being unprofessional while fairly hearing both of them out. When they argue over whether or not Patty has enough evidence to continue the case, Toomey gives them a fair compromise that satisfies both parties.
